title: Star Wars: Obi-Wan (video game)
content:

{{Otheruses|Obi-Wan}}

'''''Star Wars: Obi-Wan''''' (originally titled '''''Star Wars: Episode I: Obi-Wan''''' and scheduled for a PC-only release)<ref name="E3">{{LucasArts|url=e3/default_obiwan.htm|text=''Star Wars: Episode I: Obi-Wan'' official E3 announcement}}</ref><ref name="PC site">
{{LucasArts|url=products/obiwan|text=''Star Wars: Episode I: Obi-Wan'' (PC) Official Site}} (Cancelled)</ref> is a [[Star Wars video games|video game]] released on [[December 19]], [[2001]] for the [[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]] to tell of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi's]] tale up to and through the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]]. Many other [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] like [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]], [[Eeth Koth/Legends|Eeth Koth]], [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]], [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]], [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] participate in the game. It has villain bosses like [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], a [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] gang member, the [[Jin'ha]] Warrior Chief, and the War Chief of the [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Sand People]].

==Publisher's summary==
''Join the legendary Jedi Knight Obi-Wan Kenobi as he takes on the worst scum and villainy the galaxy has to offer in level after level of fast-paced action. In Star Wars® Obi-Wan™, you'll call upon the Force and your powerful lightsaber as you face a mysterious new enemy, an assassin droid project and a devious plot that could dramatically shift the balance of power in the universe. Fight your way through an underworld trail of munitions dealers and crime lords-from the lowest depths of Coruscant and dusty Tatooine to the lush city of Theed. The Force is in your hands.''

''The power of a legend is yours as you wield Obi-Wan's considerable Force powers, Jedi agility and lightsaber in 15+ action-packed levels.''

''Force abilities include Force attack, push, pull, throw, defend and jump.''

''Tune your skills in up to six two-player arenas—facing Jedi Masters such as Mace Windu, Ki-Adi-Mundi and Saesee Tiin.''

''Original storyline brings you face-to-face with a host of new and familiar enemies—ranging from Tusken Raiders and assassin droids, to the evil Sith Lord Darth Maul.''

==Levels {{SectionFlag|1}}==
===Chapter 1: Coruscant and the Black Heth===
====Shadows of Coruscant====
While [[Skirmishes on Coruscant|investigating]] suspicious activity in lower [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Obi-Wan encounters far more than he had anticipated.

*Liberate the transport from the [[Black Heth]]

====Saber Arena: Training Droid====
Defeat the [[JTR-47|Jedi Training Droid]] in the Jedi Training Arena.

*Defeat the Training Droid

====The Informant====
The Coruscant Guard have placed an [[Unidentified Quarren informant|informant]] inside of the Black Heth Operation. Unfortunately, he was discovered and will soon be killed. You must save him!

*Infiltrate the lower levels without setting off the alarm
*Locate the informant and free him from his thug captors

====Secrets of the Black Heth====
The Black Heth are receiving shipments of sophisticated weaponry from an unknown source. Observe their rendezvous and find out exactly who these arm dealers are.

*Defeat this new threat
*Place a tracker on the ship

===Chapter 2: Into the Den of the [[Jin'ha]]===
====Saber Arena: Saesee Tiin====
Defeat [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]] in the Jedi Training Arena.

*Defeat Saesee Tiin

====The Jin'ha Swamp====
You and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon]] must [[Second mission to Obredaan|find]] Masters [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] and [[Eeth Koth/Legends|Eeth Koth]] on [[Obredaan]].

*Locate the Jedi Masters Plo Koon and Eeth Koth

====The Mines of [[Obredaan]]====
Master Qui-Gon has gone off alone to locate the other Jedi. You must infiltrate the [[Mining/Legends|mines]] to collect evidence of the Jin'ha operation.

*Infiltrate the mines

====Escape!====
The entire Jin'ha camp has been alerted to your presence. You must get out of the mine with the evidence and rendezvous with Master Qui-Gon.

*Escape from the mine

===Chapter 3: The [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]===
====Saber Arena: Plo Koon====
Defeat Plo Koon in the Jedi Training Arena.

*Defeat Plo Koon

====Betrayal====
Your suspicions were correct. The [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] has [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|betrayed]] the trust of the Jedi Council. Fight your way to the bridge and confront the [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidians]].

*Find a way to the command deck and confront the Neimoidians

====Stowaway====
You've made it safely to one of the invasion [[Unidentified C-9979 landing craft|ships]]. You must reach the surface and disembark before the craft reaches its final destination.

*Disrupt communications to the Droid Control Ship by destroying the communication conduits
*Power down the atmospheric turbine engines

====City Under Siege====
You've commissioned a [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Tribubble bongo/Legends|craft]] to take you to the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]]. The city is under [[Capture of Theed/Legends|siege]]. You must get to the [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|Queen]] before it's too late!

*Get into the Queen's palace

====Within the Palace====
You have yet to reach the Queen but have emerged somewhere in the bowels of the palace. You must use all of your resources to make it to the throne room.

*Work with the [[Asha (Naboo)|palace servant]] to reach the throne room

====A Queen in Peril====
The Queen has been captured and will surely be forced to sign the treaty. You must save her and bring her back to Coruscant.

*Intercept the Queen and her captors and free her

===Chapter 4: Tatooine===
====Rescue on Tatooine====
While taking refuge on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], the [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] have kidnapped the Queen. You have tracked them to their dwelling...

*Locate the Queen in the Tusken Raiders' village

====The Tusken Chief====
The Queen is being held in a derelict craft that the Tusken Raiders use for shelter. You must rescue her!

*Defeat the [[Tusken war chief|Tusken War Chief]] and save the Queen

===Chapter 5: Theed===
====Saber Arena: Mace Windu====
Defeat [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] in the Jedi Saber Arena.

*Defeat Mace Windu

====Captives of the Federation====
After arriving on Naboo, a complex plan is put into motion. Work with the Naboo resistance to [[Rescue mission in Theed|launch]] an attack on the Federation Droid Ship.

*Sneak into the Detention Area
*Escort the starfighter pilots to safety

====Grounded!====
The pilots have been freed, but they cannot take off because of a large [[Quad turbolaser cannon (Trade Federation)|turbolaser cannon]] positioned just outside of the hangar. The cannon must be disabled.

*Locate and disable the turbolaser cannon

====Valuable Assistant====
[[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Prevent]] the Trade Federation from taking back the [[Theed Hangar/Legends|hangar]].

*Knock out the six power cells that control the door
*Defend the hangar from the Trade Federation forces

====Darth Maul====
You and Master Qui-Gon have fought bravely, but you're separated from the melee. And now, you can only watch.

*Defeat the evil Sith Lord [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]]

====Battle Royale====
By either completing all of the missions (including the bonus Saber Arena matches) or entering "greytherat" as your profile name, you can unlock Battle Royale. You have to defeat all the other members of the Jedi Council in a lightsaber free-for-all match.

*Defeat all the Jedi Masters.

==Trivia {{SectionFlag|1}}==
===Cheat codes/Easter eggs===
To unlock all levels, including the bonus levels, use ''greytherat'' as the player name. It also has Battle Royale, a secret bonus level.

Using the name ''M1A2U3L4!?'' activates all levels up to Darth Maul's level and allows you to view the ending clips and receive a 'bonus' mission, Battle Royale, officially.

There is an Easter egg at the end of the credits after completing the game for the first time which parodies, line for line, a quote from the movie ''Trainspotting''. Obi-Wan's voice says the following:
:Choose the Force. Choose a job. Choose the Dark Side. Choose the Council. Choose a flubbing big lightsaber. Choose protocol droids, holoprojectors, and R2 units. Choose Watto, Tatooine and Alderaan. Choose fixed-interest credit payments to Sebulba. Choose a Corellian freighter that did the Kessel run in less than twelve parsecs. Choose a Wookiee. Choose sitting in Jabba's throne room, watching mind-numbing dancers, stuffing green chubas into your mouth. Choose rotting away at the end of it all, disappearing into thin air, nothing more than an embarrassment to the whiny kid you trained on the way to Alderaan. Choose your destiny. Choose the Force. But why would I want to do a thing like that? I chose not to choose the Dark Side. I chose something else. And the reasons? There are no reasons. Who needs reasons when you've got a lightsaber?

Then an activating lightsaber is heard and the game returns to the main menu. Unfortunately you can not see this Easter egg again when you go back and re-play the final level.<ref name="Trainspotting">{{IMDb|url=title/tt0117951/quotes?qt=qt0335530|text=Trainspotting quote on IMDB}}</ref>

==Opening crawl==
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=OBI-WAN
|text=For thousands of years,
the JEDI KNIGHTS have
served the Republic as
defenders of peace and
justice throughout the
galaxy.

Young Padawan OBI-WAN
KENOBI who studies to
become a full-fledged
JEDI KNIGHT, must prove
himself to his master
QUI-GON JINN and the
Jedi Council.

As part of his ongoing
training, OBI-WAN is
commissioned by MACE
WINDU to investigate the
rise of a dangerous new
threat in the lower levels
of Coruscant....}}

==Continuity==
In the game, the Quarren informant is said to be working for the [[Coruscant Guard/Legends|Coruscant Guard]]. However, due to its timeline placement, the game may have been referring to the [[Senate Guard/Legends|Senate Guard]]. This is yet to be confirmed by a licensed source. If the player allows an alarm to be sounded, the Black Heth will execute him.
